title: Gaza Ceasefire Violations Ongoing — Phase Two Stalled
detail: As of March 20, 2026, the Gaza ceasefire framework established under Trump's 20-point peace plan remains technically in force but deeply fragile. Israeli military operations continue within Gaza with targeted strikes. Al Jazeera tracked over 2,073 Israeli ceasefire violations from October 10, 2025 through mid-March 2026, including airstrikes, shelling, and gunfire. At least 673 Palestinians were killed in the period since the October 2025 ceasefire. Phase Two of the agreement — covering Hamas disarmament and Israeli withdrawal — has not advanced, with both sides at an impasse.

media.caption: Gaza ceasefire violations ongoing as Phase Two stalls

title: Gaza Peace Phase Two Stalled — Hamas Refuses Full Disarmament
detail: Phase Two of the Gaza ceasefire — declared to have commenced on January 14, 2026 by US envoy Steve Witkoff — remains stalled as of late March 2026. Hamas refuses full disarmament of heavy weapons, insisting on Israeli withdrawal from the ~53% of Gaza under Israeli military control first. Netanyahu publicly demands full Hamas demilitarization before any Israeli withdrawal. The Trump administration has shifted its primary diplomatic focus to the Iran war, delaying Gaza Phase Two implementation. International stabilization force (ISF) plans have not materialized.
